description_long: This object is a large, floor-standing control cabinet with a white exterior and a flat top surface. The front features a set of open and closed compartments, with various laboratory equipment such as microscopes, glassware, and a Bunsen burner placed on top. Behind the cabinet is a large, mounted periodic table, suggesting a scientific or educational context. The cabinet appears to be constructed primarily from metal and wood, with some glass and plastic components visible in the equipment. The back of the cabinet is plain wood, and the sides are solid white panels. The overall design is functional, with ample surface area for supporting equipment and storage.
description: A large, white floor-standing control cabinet with a flat top, open and closed compartments, and scientific equipment on top, backed by a periodic table.
description_view_0: Front view showing open and closed compartments, scientific equipment, and a large periodic table mounted behind.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the cabinet and the arrangement of equipment on the top surface.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wooden back panel with no visible features or equipment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the alignment of equipment and the thickness of the periodic table backing.
